name: Design Principles
description: Balance, contrast, hierarchy, white space, and the iconic logos that put them to work.

questions.q: What design principle is concerned with the visual distribution of weight across a composition?
What design principle ranks visual elements by importance to guide the viewer's eye?
What design principle uses opposing elements (light/dark, big/small) to create visual interest and readability?
What is the design term for the empty area around and between elements?
What hidden element is famously embedded in negative space within the FedEx logo?

source.notes: Between the 'E' and 'x' — designed by Lindon Leader in 1994.

questions.q: Which type designer is credited with the Apple logo's bitten apple silhouette?

source.notes: Created in 1977. The bite is to distinguish from a cherry; the rainbow stripes were originally to highlight the Apple II's color graphics.

questions.q: Which graphic designer created the IBM, ABC, UPS, and Westinghouse logos?

source.notes: Rand also designed the original NeXT and ABC logos. The IBM 8-bar version is from 1972.

questions.q: Who designed the iconic 'I ♥ NY' logo in 1977?

source.notes: Glaser sketched it on the back of an envelope in a taxi.

questions.q: Who designed the Nike 'Swoosh' as a graphic design student in 1971?

source.notes: Davidson, a Portland State student, was paid $35 for the design.

questions.q: Which color-system company has been picking a 'Color of the Year' annually since 2000?
What design principle uses the recurrence of visual elements like lines, shapes, or colors?

source.notes: Closely related to rhythm and pattern.

questions.q: What design principle creates a focal point that draws the viewer's attention?
What design principle ensures all elements work together as a cohesive whole?
Which numerical ratio (~1.618), often called the 'golden ratio', shows up frequently in classical design?

source.notes: Phi ≈ 1.61803… A common compositional guide in classical art and architecture.

questions.q: Which design grid principle, popular in photography and UI, divides the canvas into 9 equal sections?

source.notes: Place key compositional elements along the lines or at their intersections.

questions.q: Which 1920s-30s German design school propagated 'form follows function' and modernist minimalism?

source.notes: Founded by Walter Gropius in Weimar, 1919. Closed by the Nazis in 1933.

questions.q: Which font, designed by Max Miedinger in 1957, is one of the most-used sans-serif typefaces in the world?

source.notes: Originally called 'Neue Haas Grotesk'; renamed Helvetica (Latin for 'Swiss') for export.

questions.q: Which Swiss typographer's 1928 manifesto 'The New Typography' codified asymmetric, sans-serif design?

source.notes: He later renounced his early modernism and embraced classical typography.

questions.q: What design movement was Apple's hardware famously associated with under Jony Ive's leadership?

source.notes: Ive's design philosophy was strongly influenced by Dieter Rams's 'Ten Principles of Good Design.'

questions.q: Whose 'Ten Principles of Good Design' (formulated at Braun in the 1970s) heavily influenced modern minimalism?
